name: business-consistency
description: Maintain NAP (Name, Address, Phone) consistency across all platforms. Use when managing citations, updating business info, or generating schema markup.
capabilities: Audit NAP consistency across all platform tiers
Track citation status from not claimed to verified
Identify and recommend fixes for inconsistencies
Create structured data for AI search engines
author: CleanExpo
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill containing only documentation and guidance for maintaining business NAP consistency. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or system access detected. All 32 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting JSON-LD schema examples as code and platform listings as reconnaissance patterns.
valueStatement: Inconsistent business information across directories damages local SEO rankings and confuses customers. This skill ensures your NAP data stays perfectly synchronized across Google Business Profile, Apple Maps, Facebook, and 30+ directories with automated audits and schema markup generation.
limitations: Does not directly update external platforms - generates recommendations only
Requires existing business_consistency_master table for full functionality
Limited to platforms documented in skill definition
Audit reports are generated in JSON format only

useCases.title: Multi-Platform NAP Audit
useCases.description: Run monthly consistency checks across Google, Bing, Apple Maps, and 50+ directories with scoring.

useCases.title: Citation Management
useCases.description: Track claim status and verify business information stays consistent after address or phone changes.

useCases.title: Schema Markup Generator
useCases.description: Automatically generate valid Schema.org JSON-LD markup from centralized business data.

promptTemplates.title: Quick NAP Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check NAP consistency for our business. Compare our master record against Google Business Profile, Bing Places, and Facebook.

promptTemplates.title: Schema Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate LocalBusiness schema markup from our business_consistency_master record for our website footer.

promptTemplates.title: Monthly Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a complete NAP audit across Tier 1-3 platforms. Report overall score, tier breakdowns, and specific issues found.

promptTemplates.title: Update Rollout
promptTemplates.prompt: Our business is changing address next week. Generate an update plan for all Tier 1-2 platforms and create new schema markup.

bestPractices: Use business_consistency_master as single source of truth for all NAP data
Run weekly spot checks on 1-2 Tier 1 platforms to catch issues early
Update schema markup immediately after any business information change
antiPatterns: Manually editing individual platform listings without updating the master record
Assuming Tier 1 platforms stay in sync automatically
Using different business names across platforms without documented reason

faq.question: Which platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Tier 1 includes Google Business Profile, Bing Places, Apple Maps, Facebook. Tier 2 adds Yellow Pages AU, True Local, Hotfrog. Tier 3 covers LinkedIn, Instagram, Twitter, YouTube.

faq.question: What is the frequency limit for audits?
faq.answer: Weekly spot checks on Tier 1 platforms recommended. Full monthly audits across all tiers for complete coverage.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my website?
faq.answer: Skill generates JSON-LD Schema.org markup from business_consistency_master table. Copy directly into website header or footer.

faq.question: Is my business data safe?
faq.answer: This is a prompt skill with no data storage. All data handling occurs through your existing Supabase database with RLS policies.

faq.question: Why does NAP consistency matter?
faq.answer: Search engines verify business legitimacy across multiple sources. Inconsistent NAP data lowers local rankings and confuses customers.

faq.question: How is this different from other citation tools?
faq.answer: Focuses on Australia-specific identifiers (ABN, ACN, QBCC) and includes GEO optimization for AI search engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT.
